name: Xoi Thap Cam (Sweet Rice with Meat, Shrimp, A
recipeIngredient: 3/4 inch pieces (available in Markets; no substitute)
1/4 lb boneless pork chops, 2 thin pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place rice in a colander and rinse under cold water.
2. In a large pot, bring water to a boil and add the rice. Stir once and let it sit for about 1 minute to ensure the water doesn't boil over. When the water starts bubbling, cover and remove from heat. Drain excess water from the pot. Return the pot to heat and cook for 20 minutes. Remove from heat, uncover, and fluff with a fork. Keep warm and fluff again right before serving.
3. For the shrimp: In a small saucepan, place dried shrimp and cold water, bring to a boil. Cover and simmer for 30 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water. Dice the shrimp and set aside.
4. Brown Chinese sausage in a dry saute pan until brown on all sides. Remove and add to the bowl with shrimp. Heat 2 teaspoons oil in the same pan and fry pork chops until brown on both sides. Add to the bowl with shrimp.
5. In a large wok over high flame, heat 2 tablespoons oil. Add scallions and cook for 3 minutes or until softened. Add pork, shrimp, and sausage; stir-fry for 1 minute. Add fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, and black pepper. Cook for 3 more minutes.
6. Re-fluff the rice and transfer to a large bowl. Add meat mixture and mix together. Press into an 8-inch round baking pan. Turn out onto serving platter and cut into 8 wedges. Sprinkle with chopped green onions and drizzle with Nuoc Cham sauce. Yield: 8 servings.
